questions.question: What happens if you run the same COPY INTO command again on files that were already loaded?

choices.A: The data loads only once; already-loaded files are skipped (idempotent)
choices.B: The data is duplicated on each run of the same COPY
choices.C: The COPY statement returns an error on already-loaded files
choices.D: Only the most recently loaded batch of rows is retained

questions.explanation: COPY tracks load history (64 days) and skips files already loaded, so repeated runs are idempotent unless FORCE = TRUE is specified.

questions.question: What is the role of the COMPRESSION parameter in a file format?

choices.A: It specifies the compression algorithm used for the files
choices.B: COMPRESSION determines the virtual warehouse size for loads
choices.C: COMPRESSION enables point-in-time recovery with Time Travel
choices.D: COMPRESSION defines the column delimiter used in files

questions.explanation: COMPRESSION tells Snowflake which algorithm (e.g., GZIP, AUTO, NONE) applies to the data files being loaded or unloaded.

questions.question: Where does the command COPY INTO @my_stage write data?

choices.A: To the named internal stage (it unloads data)
choices.B: Into a table, loading data from the COPY statement source
choices.C: To the local client disk where the SQL client runs
choices.D: To the query result cache for temporary storage

questions.explanation: COPY INTO <location> unloads table/query data to the specified stage; here, a named internal stage.

questions.question: Which file format best preserves large floating-point values precisely during load?

choices.A: PARQUET
choices.B: CSV
choices.C: Plain text
choices.D: Fixed-width text

questions.explanation: Parquet is a typed, binary columnar format that preserves numeric precision better than text formats such as CSV.

questions.question: Which practice helps optimize loading of files from external storage?

choices.A: Use appropriately sized, compressed (e.g., GZIP) files
choices.B: One very large uncompressed file to maximize throughput
choices.C: Thousands of tiny files to distribute load across nodes
choices.D: Disable file format configuration for automatic optimization

questions.explanation: Compressed, right-sized files (about 100-250 MB) reduce transfer overhead and enable parallel loading across warehouse threads.

questions.question: Which function/view retrieves errors and details about past COPY loads?

choices.A: COPY_HISTORY (and the VALIDATE function)
choices.B: QUERY_HISTORY only
choices.C: LOGIN_HISTORY
choices.D: TABLE_STORAGE_METRICS

questions.explanation: COPY_HISTORY (Information Schema/Account Usage) and the VALIDATE function expose load results and rejected-row errors.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture
questions.question: Which object lets you catalog and process unstructured files held in a stage?

choices.A: Directory table
choices.B: External table
choices.C: Stream
choices.D: Sequence

questions.explanation: A directory table layered on a stage exposes queryable file metadata, enabling cataloging and processing of unstructured data.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture
questions.question: What is the purpose of a directory table?

choices.A: To store file-level metadata (path, size, last modified) for a stage
choices.B: To store and persist table row data within a stage
choices.C: To cache and buffer query results in memory
choices.D: To apply encryption masks and hide sensitive data values

questions.explanation: Directory tables provide a queryable index of files in a stage, including each file's path, size, and timestamps.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture
questions.question: Which mechanism provides temporary, direct access to a specific staged file?

choices.A: A pre-signed URL
choices.B: A network policy
choices.C: A resource monitor
choices.D: A masking policy

questions.explanation: GET_PRESIGNED_URL generates a time-limited URL that grants direct access to a staged file for the duration of its expiration window.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture
questions.question: How can an external (non-Snowflake) application consume an individual unstructured file from a stage?

choices.A: Via a pre-signed URL
choices.B: Via COPY INTO
choices.C: Via a stream
choices.D: Via a sequence

questions.explanation: Pre-signed URLs let external tools or browsers download a staged file directly over HTTPS during the token's lifetime.

questions.question: What is the authorization behavior of a pre-signed URL?

choices.A: Anyone holding the URL can access the file until the token expires
choices.B: Only the user who issued the URL can use it; other users cannot access the file
choices.C: A pre-signed URL requires valid Snowflake authentication credentials for every access
choices.D: Pre-signed URLs never expire and remain indefinitely valid

questions.explanation: A pre-signed URL embeds time-limited credentials, so anyone with the URL can access the file until it expires; share it carefully.

questions.question: Which service powers Snowflake's built-in multi-factor authentication?

choices.A: Duo Security
choices.B: Google Authenticator
choices.C: RSA SecurID
choices.D: Okta Verify

questions.explanation: Snowflake's native MFA is provided by Duo Security; users self-enroll with no additional licensing required.

questions.question: What is the default authenticator for the Snowflake JDBC driver?

choices.A: snowflake
choices.B: externalbrowser
choices.C: oauth
choices.D: snowflake_jwt

questions.explanation: The default authenticator is 'snowflake' (username/password). Others such as externalbrowser, oauth, and snowflake_jwt must be set explicitly.

questions.question: Which authentication method requires a securely stored local private key file?

choices.A: Key-pair authentication
choices.B: Password authentication
choices.C: Duo MFA
choices.D: SAML SSO

questions.explanation: Key-pair auth uses an RSA private key stored on the client; the matching public key is registered on the Snowflake user.

questions.question: Which system role manages grants across the account by default?

choices.A: SECURITYADMIN
choices.B: SYSADMIN
choices.C: USERADMIN
choices.D: PUBLIC

questions.explanation: SECURITYADMIN holds MANAGE GRANTS and can grant/revoke privileges globally; it also inherits USERADMIN's user and role management.

questions.question: What does a role minimally need to query a secure materialized view it does not own?

choices.A: USAGE on the database/schema plus SELECT on the view
choices.B: OWNERSHIP of the secure materialized view is required for query access
choices.C: The ACCOUNTADMIN role is required to query any secure materialized view
choices.D: The MANAGE GRANTS privilege is required to query secure materialized views

questions.explanation: Following least privilege, a role needs only USAGE to traverse the containers and SELECT on the secure materialized view, not ownership or an admin role.

questions.question: Which feature reduces exposure of PII by transforming column values at query time based on the querying role?

choices.A: Dynamic Data Masking
choices.B: Resource monitors
choices.C: Result cache
choices.D: Sequences

questions.explanation: Dynamic Data Masking applies masking policies so non-privileged roles see masked values while the stored data is unchanged. (Secure views also help limit exposure.)

questions.question: Which combination best supports classifying and auditing access to sensitive data?

choices.A: Object tagging with tag-based masking policies
choices.B: Result cache and sequence generators for sensitive data classification
choices.C: Network policies only, which restrict access at the connection level
choices.D: Fail-safe recovery, which maintains historical versions of sensitive data

questions.explanation: Object tags classify sensitive columns, tag-based masking policies enforce protection consistently, and ACCESS_HISTORY audits column-level access.

questions.question: What is required for a user to begin using Snowflake MFA?

choices.A: The user must enroll themselves in MFA
choices.B: It is enabled automatically for everyone
choices.C: A separate third-party license must be purchased
choices.D: Only SSO users can enroll

questions.explanation: Snowflake MFA (Duo) requires each user to self-enroll; administrators can strongly encourage or require it, especially for privileged roles.

questions.question: Which key-pair practice lets you replace keys without an authentication outage?

choices.A: Key rotation using a second registered public key
choices.B: Disabling encryption temporarily
choices.C: Sharing the private key with the client team
choices.D: Switching to a password instead

questions.explanation: Snowflake supports two public keys (RSA_PUBLIC_KEY and RSA_PUBLIC_KEY_2) so you can rotate keys seamlessly with no downtime.

questions.question: Why might an account configure multiple identity providers (IdPs)?

choices.A: To provide different authentication for different user groups
choices.B: To disable Role-Based Access Control and rely on a single authentication method
choices.C: To increase available storage capacity and enable larger warehouse size configurations
choices.D: To bypass MFA requirements for all users and streamline the login process

questions.explanation: Multiple IdPs let an organization authenticate distinct user populations (for example employees vs. partners) through their respective providers.

questions.question: What is the minimum Snowflake edition required for periodic rekeying of data?

choices.A: Enterprise
choices.B: Standard
choices.C: Business Critical
choices.D: Virtual Private Snowflake

questions.explanation: Annual periodic rekeying of older data is available in Enterprise Edition and above. (Tri-Secret Secure, by contrast, requires Business Critical.)

questions.question: What is the minimum edition required to use Object Tagging?

choices.A: Enterprise
choices.B: Standard
choices.C: Business Critical
choices.D: Virtual Private Snowflake

questions.explanation: Object tagging, like other governance features such as masking and row access policies, requires Enterprise Edition or higher.

questions.question: At which levels can network policies be applied?

choices.A: Both the account level and the individual user level
choices.B: Network policies can be applied only at the account level without user-level overrides
choices.C: Network policies can only be applied at the individual user level, not account-wide
choices.D: Network policies can only be applied to warehouse-level access restrictions

questions.explanation: Network policies can be set account-wide and overridden per user, enabling tailored IP allow/block lists for specific users or service accounts.

questions.question: Where does data accessed through a share physically reside?

choices.A: In the provider account's micro-partitions
choices.B: Copied into the consumer's storage
choices.C: In a Snowflake-managed neutral region
choices.D: On the consumer's local disk

questions.explanation: Secure Data Sharing exposes the provider's live data in place; no copy is made, so consumers query the provider's micro-partitions.

questions.question: Which privilege does a consumer need to access an inbound share?

choices.A: IMPORT SHARE
choices.B: EXPORT SHARE
choices.C: OWNERSHIP
choices.D: MANAGE GRANTS

questions.explanation: The IMPORT SHARE privilege lets a consumer create a database from a share and access the shared, read-only objects.

questions.question: Which option lets a provider manage data sharing across a curated group of accounts?

choices.A: Data Exchange
choices.B: Reader account
choices.C: Resource monitor
choices.D: Directory table

questions.explanation: A Data Exchange provides a private hub to share data among a managed group of provider and consumer accounts.

questions.question: Setting SECURE_OBJECTS_ONLY = FALSE on a share allows sharing which additional objects?

choices.A: Regular (non-secure) views
choices.B: Warehouses
choices.C: Roles
choices.D: Resource monitors

questions.explanation: By default a share allows only secure objects; SECURE_OBJECTS_ONLY = FALSE relaxes this so standard (non-secure) views can also be shared.

questions.question: Which statements correctly describe Secure Data Sharing?

choices.A: Data is not transferred/copied, and the consumer pays for the compute used to query it
choices.B: Data is copied to the consumer and the provider pays for all compute costs
choices.C: Both parties pay to store the data twice for redundancy and safety
choices.D: Snowflake covers all storage and compute costs as a platform service

questions.explanation: Sharing provides live, read-only access without copying data; the provider stores one copy and the consumer uses its own warehouse (compute) to query it.

questions.question: What is the minimum edition required to use Secure Data Sharing?

choices.A: Standard
choices.B: Enterprise
choices.C: Business Critical
choices.D: Virtual Private Snowflake

questions.explanation: Secure Data Sharing is available beginning with Standard Edition.

questions.question: Which feature protects data availability during a regional outage?

choices.A: Cross-region replication (with failover)
choices.B: Result cache
choices.C: Time Travel
choices.D: Search Optimization Service

questions.explanation: Replicating databases to another region, combined with failover groups, provides business continuity if a region becomes unavailable.

questions.question: What is true about database replication refresh behavior?

choices.A: Only one refresh runs at a time for a given secondary
choices.B: Refreshes run continuously in parallel
choices.C: Refreshes are synchronous and block writes on the primary
choices.D: Refreshes only ever run manually

questions.explanation: Replication refreshes are asynchronous and serialized: only one refresh runs at a time per secondary, which reflects the primary as of the last completed refresh.

questions.question: Predicate pruning on a large table primarily relies on what?

choices.A: Micro-partition metadata (such as min/max values)
choices.B: The result cache used for recent query results
choices.C: Row-level B-tree indexes for data organization
choices.D: The warehouse compute size allocation

questions.explanation: Snowflake prunes by consulting per-micro-partition metadata to skip partitions that cannot contain rows matching the query predicates.

questions.question: Excessive query spillage (to local or remote storage) usually indicates what?

choices.A: The virtual warehouse is undersized for the query
choices.B: The result cache is disabled
choices.C: There are too many micro-partitions
choices.D: Storage is full

questions.explanation: Spilling means the working set exceeded available memory; scaling up to a larger warehouse with more memory typically resolves it.

questions.question: Which feature helps scale performance of occasional costly queries without permanently enlarging the warehouse?

choices.A: Query Acceleration Service
choices.B: Auto-suspend
choices.C: The Economy scaling policy
choices.D: Result cache

questions.explanation: QAS leases extra serverless compute for eligible heavy scan/filter queries, improving performance for outliers on demand.

questions.question: Which feature can improve repeated query performance over an external table?

choices.A: A materialized view defined on the external table
choices.B: Enabling AUTO_SUSPEND for external tables
choices.C: Applying a resource monitor to external tables
choices.D: Implementing a network policy for external access

questions.explanation: A materialized view pre-computes and stores results from the external table inside Snowflake, accelerating repeated queries over slower external files.

questions.question: Which Query Profile signal indicates a full table scan with poor pruning?

choices.A: Partitions scanned is close to partitions total
choices.B: The amount of bytes transmitted over network
choices.C: The total number of table joins in the query
choices.D: A successful result cache hit

questions.explanation: When partitions scanned approaches partitions total, little pruning occurred, often because the table is not clustered on the filtered columns.

questions.question: Which Query Profile operator reports partition pruning information?

choices.A: TableScan
choices.B: Sort
choices.C: Aggregate
choices.D: Result

questions.explanation: The TableScan operator reports partitions scanned versus total, showing how effectively pruning eliminated micro-partitions.

questions.question: Which object schedules recurring SQL transformations?

choices.A: Task
choices.B: Stream
choices.C: Sequence
choices.D: Stage

questions.explanation: Tasks schedule SQL statements or stored procedures (via CRON or interval) and can be chained into DAGs to build pipelines.

questions.question: Which stream metadata column identifies a changed row?

choices.A: METADATA$ROW_ID
choices.B: METADATA$FILENAME
choices.C: METADATA$STAGE
choices.D: METADATA$SIZE

questions.explanation: Streams expose METADATA$ROW_ID (row identity) along with METADATA$ACTION and METADATA$ISUPDATE to describe captured changes.

questions.question: Which actions can make a table stream stale or unusable?

choices.A: Dropping or renaming the underlying source table
choices.B: Querying the stream with SELECT
choices.C: Resizing the warehouse
choices.D: Running a SELECT on the source table

questions.explanation: A stream depends on its source table; dropping or renaming that table makes the stream stale because its offset can no longer be resolved.

questions.question: Which notations can be used to access fields in a semi-structured (VARIANT) column?

choices.A: Both dot/colon notation and bracket notation
choices.B: Only dot notation
choices.C: Only bracket notation
choices.D: Neither; you must flatten first

questions.explanation: You can navigate VARIANT data with path/dot notation (col:key) and bracket notation (col['key']).

questions.question: How can you extract the value of key 'k' from a VARIANT column c?

choices.A: Using either c:k (path) or c['k'] (bracket) notation
choices.B: Only the dot notation form c.k
choices.C: Only the bracket notation form c['k']
choices.D: Using the GET_KEY(c, 'k') function

questions.explanation: Both the path form (c:k) and the bracket form (c['k']) return the key's value as a VARIANT, which you can then cast to a target type.

questions.question: Which functions build an ARRAY from values?

choices.A: ARRAY_AGG (across rows) and ARRAY_CONSTRUCT (from arguments)
choices.B: LISTAGG aggregates values into a delimited string
choices.C: OBJECT_CONSTRUCT creates arrays from arguments
choices.D: FLATTEN builds arrays from nested structures

questions.explanation: ARRAY_AGG aggregates grouped row values into an array, while ARRAY_CONSTRUCT builds an array from explicit arguments.

questions.question: What is a key difference between loading JSON and CSV?

choices.A: CSV is flat/tabular, while JSON is hierarchical and loads into a VARIANT column
choices.B: JSON must be flattened and converted to tabular format
choices.C: CSV data is loaded directly into a VARIANT column
choices.D: JSON cannot be queried directly with standard SQL

questions.explanation: CSV maps to flat columns, whereas JSON's nested structure is stored in a VARIANT and queried with path notation and FLATTEN.

questions.question: Why load JSON into a VARIANT column?

choices.A: It preserves the nested structure and supports hierarchical querying
choices.B: VARIANT provides better data compression than all formats
choices.C: VARIANT disables schema-on-read capabilities
choices.D: VARIANT converts and stores JSON as CSV format

questions.explanation: VARIANT retains the nested JSON structure so you can query it directly with path access and FLATTEN (schema-on-read).

questions.question: By default, how does the FLATTEN function handle nested arrays?

choices.A: It flattens only the top level (RECURSIVE = FALSE by default)
choices.B: It recursively flattens all levels automatically
choices.C: It ignores arrays entirely
choices.D: It raises an error on nested arrays

questions.explanation: FLATTEN expands one level by default; set RECURSIVE => TRUE to expand nested structures at all levels.

questions.question: Which function efficiently estimates the number of distinct values?

choices.A: APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT
choices.B: COUNT(DISTINCT ...)
choices.C: ARRAY_SIZE
choices.D: TYPEOF

questions.explanation: APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT uses HyperLogLog to estimate distinct cardinality quickly and cheaply on large datasets.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture
questions.question: Which function returns the URL/location of an external stage?

choices.A: GET_STAGE_LOCATION
choices.B: GET_PRESIGNED_URL
choices.C: BUILD_SCOPED_FILE_URL
choices.D: LIST

questions.explanation: GET_STAGE_LOCATION returns the external storage location (URL) configured for a stage.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture
questions.question: How is the validity period of a pre-signed URL controlled?

choices.A: By the expiration argument passed to GET_PRESIGNED_URL
choices.B: The validity period is fixed at exactly 24 hours
choices.C: Virtual warehouse size determines the pre-signed URL expiration
choices.D: Pre-signed URLs never expire and are valid indefinitely

questions.explanation: GET_PRESIGNED_URL accepts an expiration (in seconds) argument that sets how long the generated URL remains valid.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture
questions.question: Which CLI tool is commonly used to script and automate Snowflake deployments?

choices.A: SnowSQL
choices.B: Snowsight
choices.C: Snowpark
choices.D: SnowCD

questions.explanation: SnowSQL is the command-line client used to run scripts and automate queries and deployments against Snowflake.

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture

choices.A: Snowpark

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture

questions.question: Which view shows historical query performance details?

choices.B: LOGIN_HISTORY
choices.C: TABLE_STORAGE_METRICS
choices.D: GRANTS_TO_ROLES

choices.C: QUERY_HISTORY
choices.D: STAGE_DIRECTORY

questions.domain: Snowflake AI Data Cloud Features and Architecture

choices.B: SnowSQL command-line interface exclusively

choices.D: Sequence

questions.question: What does AUTO_SUSPEND = 0 mean for a virtual warehouse?

questions.question: For Tasks, which option is correct?

questions.question: Which statement about Using Tasks with Streams is accurate?

choices.B: UDFs are distinct from stored procedures in that UDFs can be used directly within SQL queries, whereas stored procedures cannot be used as part of a SQL statement
choices.C: The result of querying a stream includes DML changes made to the source table, along with metadata such as the type of action (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
choices.D: In addition to SQL and JavaScript, you can write UDFs in Python and Java. These languages offer more flexibility for complex logic and integrations

questions.question: Which option best describes Billing Overview?

questions.question: For Credit Consumption in Multi-Cluster Warehouses, which option is correct?

choices.B: Specifies the time (in seconds) a SQL statement can remain queued on a warehouse before it is aborted
choices.C: For large tables, explicitly defining a clustering key can improve performance for range queries or filtering
choices.D: Specifies the time (in seconds) after which a running SQL statement is aborted on a warehouse

questions.explanation: Specifies the time (in seconds) after which a running SQL statement is aborted on a warehouse.

questions.question: For Caching, which option is correct for this case?

choices.A: Resizing can occur while the warehouse is running
choices.B: Cache is cleared when the warehouse is resized, suspended, or restarted
choices.C: The warehouse is running and can execute queries
choices.D: Results are invalidated if the underlying data changes

questions.explanation: Results are invalidated if the underlying data changes.

questions.question: Which statement about Clustering and Pruning is accurate?

choices.A: A multi-cluster warehouse is a group of Virtual Warehouses that can automatically scale in and out based on query demand and concurrency levels
choices.B: If a table is clustered by a date column, queries filtering on a date range will scan fewer partitions, reducing query execution time
choices.C: Snowflake uses automatic clustering to organize data into micro-partitions, improving query performance by reducing the amount of data scanned
choices.D: When a query is queued, the system estimates whether there is enough query load to keep a new warehouse busy for 6 minutes

questions.explanation: If a table is clustered by a date column, queries filtering on a date range will scan fewer partitions, reducing query execution time.

questions.question: For Search Optimization Service, which option is correct for this case?

choices.A: Data is spilled to the local SSDs of the Virtual Warehouse
choices.B: The service creates and maintains additional indexing-like metadata to enable faster lookups
choices.C: It is optional and specifically useful for queries that access small subsets of large tables
choices.D: They can be applied at the account level or to specific warehouses

questions.explanation: The service creates and maintains additional indexing-like metadata to enable faster lookups.

questions.question: Which statement about Total Micro-Partitions vs. Scanned Micro-Partitions is accurate for this case?

choices.A: Scaling up a Virtual Warehouse involves resizing it to a larger size, which can improve query performance
choices.B: Micro-partitions are optimized for storage and query performance (e.g., range metadata is stored for fast partition pruning)
choices.C: Snowflake uses partition pruning to minimize the number of micro-partitions scanned, based on query filters and clustering
choices.D: Optimization: Fewer micro-partitions scanned indicates better query performance, as irrelevant partitions are skipped

questions.explanation: Snowflake uses partition pruning to minimize the number of micro-partitions scanned, based on query filters and clustering.

questions.question: Which statement about Spilling to Local or Remote Storage is accurate?

choices.A: Data is spilled to the local SSDs of the Virtual Warehouse
choices.B: If local storage is insufficient, Snowflake spills data to its central storage layer
choices.C: Unlike cloud services, these are billed based on the actual resources consumed for the operation
choices.D: Occurs when a query requires more memory than is available in the warehouse’s allocated memory

questions.explanation: Occurs when a query requires more memory than is available in the warehouse’s allocated memory.
